w:r.w:t: Business Model: Free tier + One-time Paid Courses + Subscription Plans
User Roles: Student · Instructor · Admin · Corporate/Organization Account
First complete SRS for stakeholder review

This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) document describes the functional and non-functional requirements for EduSphere, an enterprise-grade e-learning platform. It is intended to serve as the authoritative reference for the product, design, engineering, QA, and business stakeholders throughout the software development lifecycle, and follows the structure recommended by the IEEE 830 / ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148 standards for software requirements documentation.

Requirements are uniquely identified using the prefix FR (Functional Requirement) or NFR (Non-Functional Requirement) followed by a module code and sequence number (e.g., FR-CRS-001). Priority is expressed as one of: Must Have, Should Have, Could Have, in line with the MoSCoW prioritization method. The keywords SHALL, SHOULD, and MAY are used per RFC 2119 conventions: SHALL denotes a mandatory requirement, SHOULD a recommended one, and MAY an optional one.
Product Managers and Business Analysts, to validate scope and business alignment
UI/UX Designers, to derive wireframes and design systems
Software Architects and Engineers, to design system architecture and implement features
QA Engineers, to derive test plans and acceptance criteria
Project Sponsors and Investors, to understand the scope, timeline drivers, and compliance posture
Third-party Integration Partners (payment gateways, video CDNs, SSO providers)

EduSphere is a multi-platform (Web, iOS, Android, Windows/macOS Desktop) e-learning ecosystem that enables individual instructors and corporate/organizational accounts to create, publish, and monetize educational content, while enabling students to discover, purchase or subscribe to, consume, and receive certification for that content. The platform supports a mixed monetization model: free courses, one-time paid course purchases, and recurring subscription plans (individual and organizational/team seats).
The system is designed to support the following high-level capabilities:
Multi-role account management (Student, Instructor, Admin, Corporate/Org Admin)
Course authoring, content management, and publishing workflow
Video-based, document-based, and live/webinar-based content delivery
Assessments, quizzes, assignments, and certification issuance
Payments, subscriptions, coupons, and revenue-share payouts to instructors
Corporate/organization license and seat management with team analytics
Notifications, discussion forums, and community engagement features
Administrative moderation, reporting, and analytics dashboards
AI-generated course content authoring tools (planned for a later phase)
Physical proctoring hardware integration for exams (only online proctoring via webcam is in scope)
Administrator of a Corporate/Organization account managing seats and learners
A single licensed user slot within a corporate/organization subscription
E-learning content interoperability standards for packaging course content
Content Delivery Network, used to stream video content globally
Single Sign-On (e.g., via SAML, OAuth 2.0, Google/Microsoft login)
Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 — Systems and software engineering — Requirements engineering
IEEE Std 830-1998 — Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications
WCAG 2.2 Guidelines — Web Content Accessibility Guidelines
PCI-DSS v4.0 — Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
GDPR (EU) 2016/679 and applicable regional data-protection regulations
SCORM 1.2 / 2004 and xAPI (Tin Can API) specifications

EduSphere is a new, standalone, cloud-native product. It is not a component of an existing system, though it will integrate with a defined set of external services: payment gateways, video hosting/CDN providers, email/SMS/push notification providers, SSO identity providers, and video-conferencing providers for live classes. The system is composed of a backend API layer, a shared business-logic core, and four client applications (Web, iOS, Android, Desktop) that consume the same set of APIs.
At a high level, the platform interacts with the following external actors and systems:
End Users: Students, Instructors, Admins, Corporate/Org Admins — via Web, Mobile, and Desktop clients
Payment Gateways: Stripe / Razorpay / PayPal for one-time payments and recurring subscription billing
Video Infrastructure: CDN and adaptive-bitrate streaming provider (e.g., Mux, AWS MediaConvert/CloudFront) for content storage and delivery
Live-Class Providers: WebRTC-based or third-party (e.g., Zoom SDK, Agora) for live sessions
Communication Providers: Email (transactional), SMS, and push notification services
Identity Providers: Google, Microsoft, Apple, and SAML-based SSO for enterprise customers
Analytics & BI tools: for internal reporting dashboards and data warehousing
The major functional areas covered in detail in Section 3 are summarized below:
Account creation, authentication, authorization, and role-based access control
Course catalog browsing, search, filtering, and recommendations
Course authoring and content management for instructors
Content consumption: video streaming, downloadable resources, reading materials
Assessments: quizzes, assignments, peer review, auto-grading, and certification
Commerce: cart, checkout, coupons, one-time purchases, and subscriptions
Corporate/Organization account management: bulk seats, team dashboards, license allocation
Community: discussion forums, Q&A, direct messaging, and ratings/reviews
Notifications: in-app, push, email, and SMS across all platforms
Administration: content moderation, user management, financial reporting, and system configuration
Consumes courses, takes assessments, earns certificates. May be an individual or a member of a corporate seat.
Authors and publishes courses, manages pricing, tracks earnings and learner engagement.
Manages an organizational account: purchases seats/licenses, assigns learners, tracks team progress and compliance.
Internal staff managing platform-wide configuration, content moderation, payouts, and compliance.
Unauthenticated user browsing the public catalog and marketing pages before registering.
Web Client: Latest two major versions of Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge on Windows, macOS, and Linux
Desktop Clients: Windows 10/11 and macOS 12+, distributed via a native wrapper (e.g., Electron or platform-native shell) sharing the web codebase where feasible
Backend: Cloud-hosted (AWS/GCP/Azure), containerized microservices, managed relational and NoSQL databases, object storage for media, and a CDN edge network
Offline Support: Mobile and Desktop clients SHALL support downloading course videos and materials for offline viewing within DRM constraints
The system SHALL comply with PCI-DSS requirements for any component that touches card data; card data itself SHALL NOT be stored on EduSphere servers and SHALL be tokenized via the payment gateway
Video content delivery SHALL use adaptive bitrate streaming (HLS/DASH) to support varying network conditions
The platform SHALL support horizontal scaling to accommodate seasonal enrollment spikes (e.g., back-to-school periods, corporate fiscal year-end training pushes)
All client applications SHALL consume a common versioned REST/GraphQL API to minimize platform-specific business logic drift
Content protection (DRM/watermarking) SHALL be applied to paid video content to deter piracy
Users have access to a stable internet connection for initial content download/streaming; offline mode is a secondary, not primary, mode of use
Third-party payment gateways and video-CDN providers maintain their own published SLAs; platform SLAs are dependent on these upstream services
Corporate customers will provide a designated Org Admin responsible for internal license allocation and data-privacy agreements
Content ratings/moderation policies assume instructors upload original or appropriately licensed content, subject to platform review
This section details the functional requirements grouped by module. Each module includes a brief description followed by a requirements table with a unique ID, description, and MoSCoW priority.
Covers registration, login, role-based access control, and profile management across all four user classes.
The system SHALL allow registration via email/password, and SSO (Google, Apple, Microsoft, SAML for enterprise).
The system SHALL support email verification and phone number verification (OTP) during onboarding.
The system SHALL enforce role-based access control (RBAC) for Student, Instructor, Admin, and Org Admin roles, with a single user able to hold multiple roles (e.g., Instructor + Student).
The system SHALL support optional Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all roles and SHALL mandate MFA for Admin and Org Admin roles.
The system SHALL provide password reset via secure, time-limited email links.
Users SHALL be able to manage their profile: name, photo, bio, timezone, language preference, and notification preferences.
The system SHALL support account deactivation and a GDPR/CCPA-compliant data deletion request workflow.
Corporate/Org Admins SHALL be able to provision learner accounts in bulk via CSV upload or SAML-based auto-provisioning (SCIM).
Covers course discovery, enrollment, learning experience, and progress tracking for learners.
Students SHALL be able to browse and search the public course catalog without an account (guest browsing).
Students SHALL be able to enroll in free courses instantly and in paid courses upon successful checkout.
Students SHALL be able to track per-course and per-lesson progress, including percentage completion and last-viewed position (resume playback).
Students SHALL be able to bookmark lessons, take notes, and download supplementary materials (PDF, slides) where permitted by the instructor.
Students SHALL be able to download course videos for offline viewing on mobile/desktop clients, subject to DRM and instructor permission.
Students SHALL be able to rate and review completed courses.
Students SHALL receive a personalized dashboard showing enrolled courses, upcoming live sessions, deadlines, and recommended next courses.
Students SHALL be able to view and download earned certificates in PDF format with a unique verification link/QR code.
Students who are part of a corporate/org account SHALL see org-assigned mandatory courses distinctly from self-selected courses.
Covers course authoring, publishing workflow, learner engagement tools, and earnings management for instructors.
Instructors SHALL be able to apply for an instructor account, subject to Admin approval and identity verification.
Instructors SHALL be able to create courses composed of sections and lessons, each lesson supporting video, article/text, downloadable file, or quiz content types.
Instructors SHALL be able to upload video content that is automatically transcoded into multiple resolutions for adaptive streaming.
Instructors SHALL be able to set course pricing as free, one-time paid, or included-in-subscription, and SHALL be able to create limited-time discount coupons.
Instructors SHALL be able to submit courses for Admin review prior to public listing, and SHALL receive feedback on rejection.
Instructors SHALL be able to schedule and host live classes/webinars integrated into a course.
Instructors SHALL have access to an analytics dashboard showing enrollments, completion rates, revenue, and learner ratings per course.
Instructors SHALL be able to respond to student questions via course-level Q&A/discussion threads.
Instructors SHALL be able to view and withdraw earnings according to the platform's revenue-share schedule and payout cycle.
Instructors SHALL be able to duplicate, archive, or unpublish their own courses.
Covers team/enterprise licensing, seat management, and compliance tracking for organizational customers.
Org Admins SHALL be able to purchase a license plan defining a total number of seats and included course catalog access.
Org Admins SHALL be able to invite, assign, and revoke seats for individual learners via email invite, bulk CSV import, or SSO-based provisioning.
Org Admins SHALL be able to assign mandatory/compliance training with due dates to individuals or groups.
Org Admins SHALL be able to view a team dashboard showing per-learner and aggregate completion rates, quiz scores, and certification status.
Org Admins SHALL be able to export compliance and completion reports (CSV/PDF) for audit purposes.
The system SHALL support tiered organizational roles (Org Admin, Org Manager with limited scope, Org Learner).
The system SHALL support custom branding (logo, color theme) on the organization's private learning portal (white-labeling).
The system SHALL provide keyword search across course title, description, instructor name, and tags, with typo-tolerance.
The system SHALL support filtering by category, level, price, language, rating, and duration.
The system SHALL provide a recommendation engine suggesting courses based on browsing history, enrolled courses, and peer learner behavior.
The system SHALL support curated learning paths: ordered sequences of multiple courses toward a skill or certification track.
The system SHALL support multiple-choice, true/false, fill-in-the-blank, and short-answer quiz question types with auto-grading.
The system SHALL support instructor-graded assignments with file upload submissions and rubric-based scoring.
The system SHALL support peer-review assignments where learners grade each other's submissions against a rubric.
The system SHALL issue a digital certificate automatically upon meeting course completion criteria (e.g., minimum quiz score, 100% lesson completion).
Certificates SHALL include a unique verification code/URL allowing third parties to validate authenticity.
The system SHOULD support basic online proctoring (webcam snapshot capture, tab-switch detection) for high-stakes assessments.
The system SHALL support a shopping cart allowing multiple one-time course purchases in a single checkout.
The system SHALL integrate with third-party payment gateways supporting credit/debit cards, digital wallets, and regional payment methods.
The system SHALL support recurring subscription billing (monthly/annual) for individual learners with automatic renewal and cancellation.
The system SHALL support corporate invoicing and purchase-order based billing in addition to card payments for Org accounts.
The system SHALL support coupon codes, referral discounts, and regional/currency-based pricing.
The system SHALL generate invoices/receipts automatically for every transaction and make them downloadable.
The system SHALL calculate and schedule instructor revenue-share payouts according to configurable rules (e.g., platform fee percentage).
The system SHALL support refund requests within a configurable policy window (e.g., 14 days), routed through Admin approval.
The system SHALL allow instructors to schedule live sessions with calendar invites and automated reminders.
The system SHALL support live video/audio, screen-sharing, and text chat during live sessions.
The system SHALL automatically record live sessions and make replays available to enrolled learners.
The system SHALL support breakout rooms and live polling during sessions.
The system SHALL provide a per-course discussion/Q&A forum visible to enrolled learners and the instructor.
The system SHALL support upvoting of questions/answers and instructor-marked "best answer" tagging.
The system SHALL support direct, moderated messaging between learners and instructors.
The system SHALL flag and route reported content/messages to Admin for moderation review.
The system SHALL send transactional notifications (enrollment confirmation, payment receipt, certificate issuance) via email.
The system SHALL send push notifications on mobile and desktop clients for reminders, new content, and messages, per user preference.
The system SHALL provide an in-app notification center with read/unread status.
Corporate accounts SHALL be able to send bulk announcements/reminders to assigned learners for compliance deadlines.
Admins SHALL be able to approve, reject, suspend, or unpublish courses and instructor accounts.
Admins SHALL be able to manage platform-wide configuration: categories, tax rules, revenue-share percentages, and supported payment methods.
Admins SHALL have access to a global reporting dashboard: revenue, active users, enrollment trends, and top-performing courses.
Admins SHALL be able to review and act on flagged/reported content and user complaints.
Admins SHALL be able to issue manual refunds and adjust instructor payouts with an audit trail.
The system SHALL maintain an immutable audit log of all administrative actions.
The system SHALL provide a responsive Web application supporting desktop and tablet breakpoints.
The system SHALL provide native iOS and Android mobile applications following each platform's Human Interface Guidelines / Material Design conventions respectively.
The system SHALL provide a Desktop application (Windows/macOS) for offline-capable learning and distraction-free content consumption.
All interfaces SHALL conform to WCAG 2.2 Level AA accessibility guidelines, including keyboard navigation, screen-reader support, and captioning for video content.
The system SHALL support a minimum of English at launch, with an internationalization (i18n) framework in place for adding additional languages.
Mobile clients SHALL interface with device cameras and microphones for live-class participation and webcam-based proctoring.
Desktop/mobile clients SHALL interface with local device storage for offline-downloaded course content, subject to available storage checks.
Process one-time payments, recurring subscriptions, and payouts
Video Processing/CDN (e.g., Mux, AWS Elemental, Cloudflare Stream)
Transcode, store, and stream adaptive-bitrate video content
Live/Video-Conferencing SDK (e.g., Agora, Zoom SDK, WebRTC)
Power live classes, screen sharing, and recordings
Product analytics, funnel tracking, and business reporting
Regional tax calculation and compliant invoice generation
All client-server communication SHALL occur over HTTPS/TLS 1.2 or higher.
The system SHALL expose a versioned REST or GraphQL API used consistently by all client applications.
Real-time features (live chat, notifications, live-class signaling) SHALL use WebSocket or equivalent persistent-connection protocols.
95% of API responses SHALL complete within 500ms under normal load (excluding video streaming/transcoding operations).
Video playback SHALL begin within 3 seconds of a learner pressing play under a broadband connection (≥5 Mbps).
The system SHALL support at least 100,000 concurrent active learners at general availability, scaling horizontally thereafter.
Search queries SHALL return results within 1 second for catalogs of up to 1 million courses.
The system SHALL encrypt data at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+).
The system SHALL NOT store raw payment card data; all card handling SHALL be tokenized via a PCI-DSS compliant payment processor.
The system SHALL apply DRM/watermarking to paid video content to deter unauthorized redistribution.
The system SHALL enforce rate limiting and bot/abuse protection on authentication and checkout endpoints.
The system SHALL undergo periodic third-party penetration testing (at minimum annually) prior to and after major releases.
The system SHALL maintain role-scoped data access so that Org Admins can only view data for their own organization's learners.
A first-time learner SHALL be able to complete registration and enroll in a free course within 3 minutes without external help.
The system SHALL provide contextual onboarding tours for each of the four user roles.
All interfaces SHALL conform to WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility standards.
The platform SHALL maintain a minimum of 99.9% uptime for core learning and checkout services, measured monthly.
The system SHALL implement automated failover and daily backups for all persistent data stores, with a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of 24 hours and Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 4 hours.
The backend SHALL be built as independently deployable services/modules to allow scaling of high-traffic areas (e.g., video delivery, search) independently of others.
The codebase SHALL maintain automated test coverage of at least 70% for core business logic, enforced via CI/CD pipelines.
Core business logic SHALL be decoupled from any single cloud provider's proprietary services where practical, to support future multi-cloud portability.
The system SHALL comply with GDPR for EU users and CCPA for California users, including data access/export/deletion rights.
The system SHALL comply with COPPA-equivalent safeguards where the platform is used by, or markets to, minors (e.g., K-12 corporate/educational accounts).
The system SHALL comply with PCI-DSS for all payment-processing touchpoints.
The system SHOULD support SCORM/xAPI-compliant content import/export to support enterprise LMS interoperability.
This section provides a high-level, implementation-agnostic view of the proposed architecture to guide the engineering team's detailed design.
Client Layer: Web (responsive SPA), iOS app, Android app, Desktop app — all consuming a common versioned API.
API Gateway Layer: Handles authentication, rate limiting, request routing, and API versioning.

Service Layer (domain-oriented services): Identity & Access, Course Catalog, Content Delivery, Assessment & Certification, Commerce & Billing, Organization/Enterprise Management, Notifications, Community/Discussion, and Reporting/Analytics.

Data Layer: Relational database for transactional data (users, courses, orders, enrollments), a search index (e.g., Elasticsearch/OpenSearch) for catalog search, object storage for media assets, and a data warehouse for analytics.
Media Pipeline: Upload intake, transcoding/adaptive-bitrate packaging, CDN distribution, and DRM/watermarking.
Integration Layer: Adapters for payment gateways, SSO providers, video-conferencing SDKs, and communication providers.
Domain-oriented service boundaries to allow independent scaling and deployment (e.g., media delivery scales independently of billing).
API-first design: all clients are equal consumers of the same public/internal API surface, avoiding platform-specific backend logic.
Event-driven communication between services for cross-cutting concerns (e.g., "course completed" event triggers certificate issuance and notification services).
Security-by-design: role-based access enforced at the API gateway and service layer, not solely in client applications.
The following table summarizes the core data entities and their primary attributes. This is a conceptual model intended to inform detailed database design, not an exhaustive schema.
id, name, email, role(s), password_hash/SSO_id, timezone, locale, status
id, name, plan_type, seat_count, billing_info, branding_config
id, instructor_id, title, description, category, level, price_type, price, status, language
id, course_id, order, title, content_type, video_ref, duration, resources[]
id, user_id, course_id, source(self/org-assigned), progress_pct, status, enrolled_date
id, user_id/org_id, items[], amount, currency, payment_status, invoice_ref
id, user_id/org_id, plan, billing_cycle, status, renewal_date
id, user_id, course_id, rating, comment, created_date
id, user_id, type, channel, status, created_date
A representative (non-exhaustive) list of primary use cases, grouped by actor, to be expanded into detailed use-case specifications and acceptance criteria during sprint planning.
UC-01: Browse and search course catalog as a guest
UC-02: Register and verify a new account
UC-03: Purchase a course or subscribe to a plan
UC-04: Watch a video lesson with progress auto-saved
UC-05: Take a quiz and receive an immediate auto-graded result
UC-06: Download and share an earned certificate
UC-07: Apply for and be approved as an instructor
UC-08: Author a new course with sections, lessons, and a quiz
UC-09: Submit a course for review and publish it
UC-10: Host a live class and review the recording afterward
UC-11: View earnings dashboard and request a payout
UC-12: Purchase a seat-based license plan for the organization
UC-13: Bulk-import learners and assign mandatory training
UC-14: Monitor team-wide completion and compliance dashboard
UC-15: Export an audit-ready compliance report
UC-16: Review and approve/reject a submitted course
UC-17: Investigate and resolve a flagged discussion post
UC-18: Configure platform-wide revenue-share and tax rules
UC-19: Issue a manual refund and adjust an instructor payout
Pricing, revenue-share percentages, and subscription tiers described here are illustrative and subject to finalization by the business/finance team.
The initial release (MVP) may de-scope lower-priority ("Could Have") items listed in Section 3 and Section 5; these are documented here to preserve the full long-term vision.
Video piracy/content leakage undermining instructor trust
Use PCI-DSS compliant gateways with built-in fraud detection; manual review thresholds
Mandatory course-review workflow before publishing; ratings-driven visibility
Regulatory risk in multiple jurisdictions (data residency, minors' data)
Region-aware data storage and compliance review per target market
Adaptive bitrate encoding, CDN caching, and storage lifecycle policies
AI-assisted course content generation and auto-captioning/translation
Adaptive/personalized learning paths driven by ML-based skill-gap analysis
Marketplace for third-party plugins/integrations (e.g., HR systems, external LMS)

This SRS is considered a living document. Material changes to scope, priority, or architecture SHALL be recorded in the Revision History table and re-circulated for stakeholder approval before implementation begins.
